nodes.name: Replay Entry
nodes.notes: Contract B replay entry. Incident events can be tenant-agnostic; 05-dlq-replay allows that workflow explicitly.

parameters.jsCode:
const p = $('Normalize Incident').first().json;
return [{ json: { key: p.idem_key, tenant_id: p.tenant_id, workflow: '08-incident-comms' } }];

const p = $('Normalize Incident').first().json;
return [{ json: { tenant_id: p.tenant_id, correlation_id: p.correlation_id, service: 'incident', step: 'ingest', status: 'skipped', payload: { reason: 'duplicate event', key: p.idem_key, monitor_id: p.monitor_id, status: p.incident_status } } }];

const p = $('Normalize Incident').first().json;
return [{ json: { tenant_id: p.tenant_id, correlation_id: p.correlation_id, service: 'incident', step: 'ingest', status: 'started', payload: { source: p.replay ? 'replay' : 'uptime-kuma', monitor_id: p.monitor_id, monitor_name: p.monitor_name, status: p.incident_status, region: p.region, execution_id: p.execution_id } } }];

nodes.name: Fetch Impacted Tenants
nodes.notes: fullResponse keeps a single wrapper item even when the tenant list is [] (an empty array body would otherwise split into zero items and starve the rest of the workflow). Same pattern as 02-idempotency-check.
